metadata.title:
HAVE CONFIDENCE


IN MRS. HOUSE

LEADER IN POLICE MATRON
FIGHT REELECTED PRESl.
DENT OF THE WOMAN'S


CLUB.

pages.raw_text:
The faithful and untiring efforts
of Mrs. A. E. House as president of
the Woman's club were rewarded
last night by her reelection to the
post of presiding officer by a vote
that was almost unanimous.


Mrs. House has led in the fight
for police matronS at the city Jail,
and because of her activity in this
movement and the further fact that
she went on the bond of Elizabeth
Gurley Flynn in the recent street
speaking campaign. some women
in sympathy with the city hall tried
to defeat her reelection.


The opposition to Mrs. House was
led by Mrs. j. r. Cassin and Mrs.
Leo Brown, but the count of the
vote showed that Mrs. House re-
ceived so of the Sc votes present.


Mrs. House is honest and fear
less. If she thinks a question is
right she is not afraid to take a
stand even if President Taft led
the opposition. She has done won
ders for the Womans club, and
that the women are appreciative
was demonstrated by the vote of
confidence given last night


Other officers are:


Mrs. Praeger, first vice presi-
dent; Mrs. D. A. Stewart, second;
Mrs. w. G. Gleason, third; Mrs.
Lucy Hall, recording secretary;
Mrs. Rowena Brown, financial sec
rotary; Mrs. A. P. Fassett, treas
urer, and Miss Katie Wetherill, cor
responding secretary.


The trustees were Mrs. Prayer,
Mrs. Lucy Hall, Mrs. Gleason, Mrs.
A. p. Fassett, Mrs. d. A. Stewart,
Mrs. Brown, Mrs. A. E. House, Mrs.
Power. Miss Wetherill, Mrs. Will
liam Bieber, Mrs. o. B. Settlers,
Mrs. Paxson, Mrs. Saxton, Mrs. Lee
and Mrs. Peterson.


The board met yesterday at 2:30
had a recess from $3.50 to 1880, and
did not close the session until II
o'clock p. m.
